---
title: "Paweł Morzywołek"
subtitle: Section of Biostatistics <br> University of Copenhagen
image: PM2.jpg
resources: MasterThesis-PawelMorzywolek.pdf, RandomTrees-BachelorThesis.pdf, cv.pdf
about: 
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - text: GitHub
      icon: github
      href: https://github.com/pmorzywolek
    - text: LinkedIn
      icon: linkedin
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/pawel-morzywolek-50071bb4/
    - text: CV
      icon: file-text-fill
      href: cv.pdf
    - text: Email
      icon: envelope-fill
      href: mailto:pawel.morzywolek@sund.ku.dk
---

# Biography

I am an assistant professor of biostatistics in the Department of Public Health at the [University of Copenhagen](https://publichealth.ku.dk/about-the-department/biostat/). My research centers on causal and statistical inference, with a particular focus on infinite-dimensional parameters and their applications to the study of common complex diseases. I am also affiliated with the [SMARTbiomed Pioneer Center](https://smartbiomed.dk/about-smartbiomed).

Previously, I was a postdoctoral fellow in the Department of Statistics at the [University of Washington](https://stat.uw.edu/), where I worked with [Alex Luedtke](http://www.alexluedtke.com/) and [Peter Gilbert](https://www.fredhutch.org/en/faculty-lab-directory/gilbert-peter.html) on inference for local variable importance measures applied to evaluating the efficacy of infectious disease prevention strategies. During this time, I was also a [UW Data Science](https://escience.washington.edu/about/) postdoctoral fellow and a [PIMS-Simons](https://www.pims.math.ca/) postdoctoral fellow.

I obtained my PhD in Statistical Data Analysis from [Ghent University](https://www.ugent.be/en) under the supervision of [Johan Steen](https://research.ugent.be/web/person/johan-steen-0/en), [Wim Van Biesen](https://research.ugent.be/web/person/wim-van-biesen-0/en), and [Stijn Vansteelandt](https://users.ugent.be/~svsteela/). My doctoral research applied causal inference methods to determine the optimal timing of treatment initiation in critically ill patients using observational, routinely collected data.

I hold a degree in mathematics from [ETH Zurich](https://ethz.ch/en.html), where I specialized in statistical learning theory and mathematical finance. Beyond academia, I have served as a teaching assistant at the African Institute for Mathematical Sciences ([AIMS South Africa](https://aims.ac.za/)) and worked as an actuary and a data scientist at a consulting company [EY](https://www.ey.com/en_ch).

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Research interests
- Causal inference 
- Semiparametric theory
- Statistical machine learning
- Biostatistics
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Education
<ul class="fa-ul">
  <li><span class="fa-li">{{< fa landmark >}}</span>PhD in Statistical Data Analysis, 2023 <br /> Ghent University </li>
  <li><span class="fa-li">{{< fa landmark >}}</span>MSc in Mathematics, 2015 <br /> ETH Zurich </li>
  <li><span class="fa-li">{{< fa landmark >}}</span>BSc in Mathematics, 2014 <br /> ETH Zurich </li>
</ul>
:::

::::
